description: private university in Hanover, New Hampshire, US
description_wiki: Dartmouth College ( DART-məth) is a private Ivy League research university in Hanover, New Hampshire, United States.

description: town in Grafton County, New Hampshire, United States
description_wiki: Hanover is a town located along the Connecticut River in Grafton County, New Hampshire, United States.

description: private school in Japan
description_wiki: The American School in Japan (ASIJ; Japanese: アメリカンスクール・イン・ジャパン) is an international private day school located in the city of Chōfu, Tokyo, Japan.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: special ward in the Tokyo Metropolis in Japan
description_wiki: Shibuya (渋谷区, Shibuya-ku; IPA: [ɕibɯja] ) is a special ward in Tokyo, Japan.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

surface_forms: President Hopkins of Dartmouth was a personal friend of FDR
description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: American educator (1877–1964); 11th president of Dartmouth College
description_wiki: Ernest Martin Hopkins (November 6, 1877 – August 13, 1964) served as the 11th President of Dartmouth College from 1916 to 1945.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: Savage, Minnesota, the former site of a Military Intelligence Service language school during World War II
description_wiki: Camp Savage is the former site of the U.S. Military Intelligence Service language school operating during World War II. The school itself was established in San Francisco, but was moved in 1942 to Savage, Minnesota.

description: city in and county seat of Salt Lake County, Utah, United States and the capital of Utah
description_wiki: Salt Lake City, often shortened to Salt Lake or SLC, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Utah.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: former American military unit
description_wiki: The Military Intelligence Service (Japanese: アメリカ陸軍情報部, America Rikugun Jōhōbu) was a World War II U.S. military unit consisting of two branches, the Japanese American unit (described here) and the German-Austrian unit based at Camp Ritchie, best known as the "Ritchie Boys".

description: land warfare branch of Australia's defence forces
description_wiki: The Australian Army is the principal land warfare force of Australia.

description: joint Australian/American intelligence agency that translated intercepted Japanese communications during World War II
description_wiki: The Allied Translator and Interpreter Section (ATIS), also known as the Allied Translator and Interpreter Service or Allied Translator and Intelligence Service, was a joint Australian/American World War II intelligence agency which served as a centralized allied intelligence unit for the translation of intercepted Japanese communications, interrogations and negotiations in the Pacific Theater of Operations between September 1942 and  December 1945.

description: capital of Papua New Guinea
description_wiki: Port Moresby ( ; Tok Pisin: Pot Mosbi), also referred to as Pom City or simply Moresby, is the capital and largest city of Papua New Guinea.

description: island in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: New Guinea (Tok Pisin: Niugini; Hiri Motu: Niu Gini; Indonesian: Papua, fossilized Nugini, also known as Papua or historically Irian) is the world's second-largest island, with an area of 785,753 km2 (303,381 sq mi).

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Hollandia may refer to:

description: island in Biak Numfor Regency, Papua Province, Indonesia
description_wiki: Biak is the main island of Biak Archipelago located in Cenderawasih Bay near the northern coast of Papua, an Indonesian province, and is just northwest of New Guinea.

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

aliases: Second Battle of the Philippine Sea
description: 1944 the largest naval battle of World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of Leyte Gulf (23–26 October 1944) was the largest naval battle of World War II and by some criteria the largest naval battle in history, with over 200,000 naval personnel involved.

description: second largest island of the Philippines
description_wiki: Mindanao ( , MIN-də-NOW) is the second-largest island in the Philippines, after Luzon, and seventh-most populous island in the world.

description: formation of the United States Army
1941-1996 combat formation of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 24th Infantry Division was an infantry division of the United States Army that was deactivated in October 1996.

aliases: Army of the Greater Japanese Empire
description: ground-based armed forces of Japan, from 1868 to 1945
description_wiki: The Imperial Japanese Army (IJA) was the principal ground force of the Empire of Japan from 1871 to 1945.

description: type of torpedo-armed fast attack craft

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

entity: International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement
description: international humanitarian movement
description_wiki: The International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement is a humanitarian movement with approximately 16 million volunteers, members, and staff worldwide.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Switzerland, officially the Swiss Confederation, is a landlocked country located at the intersection of Central, Western, and Southern Europe.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: city in Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Fukuyama (福山市, Fukuyama-shi) is a city in Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan.

description: city in Kanagawa prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kamakura (鎌倉, Kamakura; [kamakɯɾa] ), officially Kamakura City (鎌倉市, Kamakura-shi), is a city of Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan.

description: main residence of the Emperor of Japan
description_wiki: The Imperial Palace (皇居, Kōkyo; lit. 'Imperial Residence') is the main residence of the emperor of Japan.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.
